plan_metadata.name: DA Add Action Import Existing API Basic OAuth

steps.description: Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the "New Project" modal of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "No Action" option from the dropdown menu under "Add an action to your declarative agent" in the "Create Declarative Agent" window of Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit.
Click the "Start with an OpenAPI Description Document" option in the "Create an Action" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Click on the input field labeled "Enter OpenAPI Description Document Location or Open File..." in the OpenAPI Spec Document dialog within Visual Studio Code.
Click the text input field labeled "Enter OpenAPI Description Document Location" in the OpenAPI Description Document prompt within Visual Studio Code's Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click on the "Enter OpenAPI Description Document URL" input field in the OpenAPI Description Document dialog within Visual Studio Code.
Type the OpenAPI specification URL into the "Enter OpenAPI Description Document URL" input field in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension for Visual Studio Code.
Press Enter to confirm the URL input in the "OpenAPI Description Document" address bar in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "OK" button in the "Select Operation(s) Copilot Can Interact with" dialog at the top of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the blue "Clear All" button in the dropdown for operation selection within the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" extension in Visual Studio Code, to deselect all chosen APIs.
Click the "Default folder" option in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select '/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects' as the project location in Visual Studio Code.
Type 'fake_client_id' into the "Oauth registration client ID" input field in the Visual Studio Code extension interface during the Provision step for a Microsoft 365 agent.
Press "Enter" to confirm the input "fake_client_id" in the "OAuth registration client ID" dialog within Visual Studio Code.
Type text: 'fake_clientsecret' into the "OAuth registration client secret" input field in the Visual Studio Code extension interface during the agent provisioning workflow.
Press 'Enter' to submit the OAuth registration client secret in the input dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface during the agent provisioning workflow.
Click the "Confirm" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit OAuth registration confirmation dialog within Visual Studio Code to grant access and continue the provisioning process.
@assertion there is a button containing "Sign in to Repair Service"
